summary: All AI inference (STT, LLM completion, embeddings, RAG) runs fully on-device via @qvac/sdk. No user data — audio, transcripts, embeddings, graph, or derived content — ever leaves the device. There are zero cloud AI/inference API calls.

remote_calls.name: QVAC model weight download (Hugging Face registry)
remote_calls.purpose: One-time download of model weights on first launch, performed by the @qvac/sdk loader from the model sources declared in src/qvac/models.ts (registry://hf/...). Weights are cached locally; subsequent runs are fully offline.
remote_calls.notes: Models can be pre-provisioned for a fully offline demo. No AI computation happens over the network; only static weight files are fetched.

peer_to_peer_sharing.name: AirDrop / iOS share sheet (.memit bundles)
peer_to_peer_sharing.purpose: Optional device-to-device sharing of a single meeting bundle (transcript, summary, action items, chunk embeddings, graph subgraph) via expo-sharing / expo-document-picker. Uses Apple AirDrop (Bluetooth + direct Wi-Fi) — no server, no internet, no cloud.
peer_to_peer_sharing.user_data_sent: only the meeting the user explicitly chooses to share, and only to the device they select
peer_to_peer_sharing.notes: No remote API. Transport is peer-to-peer over the OS share sheet.

telemetry_or_analytics: none added by mem-it. Standard Expo / React Native runtime may perform framework-level network activity outside the app's control; the app itself adds no analytics, crash reporting, or tracking.
notes: Storage (expo-sqlite) and file access (expo-file-system) are entirely local to the device.
